label: Vulnerability Management Engineer
description: Designs and operates vulnerability management programs covering scanning, risk-based prioritization, remediation tracking, and reporting across enterprise and cloud environments.

paths.name: Tenable Platform Track
paths.rationale: For VM engineers on the Tenable platform. Tenable (Nessus) is the most widely deployed vulnerability scanning platform globally. Specialist certification validates platform-specific depth that employers running Tenable directly look for.
pros: Tenable certifications are recognized at Tenable customer organizations
CySA+ adds DoD 8140 approved vendor-neutral VM credential
Tenable platform covers on-prem and cloud environments
cons: Platform-specific — limited applicability outside Tenable environments

prerequisites.experience: IT operations or security operations experience beneficial. Hands-on Tenable access (trial or employer environment) is essential.

cert_sequence.notes: CompTIA CySA+ provides the DoD 8140 approved vendor-neutral VM and detection foundation. Establishes vulnerability management methodology concepts before platform-specific Tenable certification.

Tenable Vulnerability Management Specialist — both the written and practical exam components required. 80% passing threshold on both. Study through Tenable University's official curriculum. Hands-on platform access is mandatory — the practical exam requires actual vulnerability management workflows in the Tenable.io or Tenable.sc environment.

Tenable Vulnerability Management Expert — advanced platform credential covering complex scan policies, dashboard creation, API integration, and enterprise-scale VM workflows. Contact Tenable directly for Expert-level exam availability and pricing. At this level you're demonstrating deep Tenable platform mastery beyond standard deployment.

paths.name: Qualys Platform Track
paths.rationale: For VM engineers on the Qualys platform. Qualys is the second most widely deployed VM platform globally, dominant in cloud and SaaS environments. All Qualys certifications are free.
pros: All Qualys certifications are free — lowest cost path possible
Covers VMDR, CSAM, cloud agent, and TotalCloud for broad platform coverage
CySA+ adds DoD 8140 approved vendor-neutral foundation
cons: Non-proctored, open-book — limited employer credentialing weight

prerequisites.experience: Qualys platform access (free trial available) for hands-on practice.

cert_sequence.notes: Vendor-neutral DoD-approved VM and detection foundation. The only cost in this path.
Qualys VMDR (Vulnerability Management, Detection and Response) certification — free, covers the core Qualys VM workflow. Complete Qualys's free training on Qualys University before the assessment.
Qualys Cyber Security Asset Management — free certification covering asset discovery and inventory management. CSAM + VMDR together provide the full asset-to-vulnerability workflow coverage.

Qualys TotalCloud — cloud-native vulnerability and security posture management. Extends VM skills into cloud environments where traditional scan-based approaches need supplementing with CSPM capabilities.

paths.name: GIAC / Vendor-Neutral Track
paths.rationale: For vulnerability management engineers who need platform-independent expertise and DoD recognition. GEVA is the only dedicated GIAC vulnerability management credential. Paired with CySA+ for DoD baseline and CRISC for the risk management framework that elevates VM from a scanning function to a risk governance program.
pros: GEVA is the only dedicated GIAC VM credential — DoD approved
CRISC elevates VM practitioners from technicians to risk-informed program managers
CySA+ adds broad threat analysis context to vulnerability prioritization
Vendor-neutral stack applicable across all scanning platforms
cons: CRISC requires 3 years IT risk management experience
GEVA has lower employer name recognition than tool-specific certs at some organizations

prerequisites.experience: IT operations or security operations background recommended. CRISC requires 3 years risk management experience.

cert_sequence.notes: CompTIA CySA+ — DoD approved threat analysis and vulnerability management foundation. Covers the analytical methodology for interpreting vulnerability scan results and prioritizing remediation based on threat context.

GIAC Enterprise Vulnerability Assessor — the only dedicated GIAC VM certification. Covers enterprise vulnerability assessment methodology, scanning tool operation, risk-based prioritization, and remediation program management. DoD approved. Open-book exam — build notes indexed by assessment phase.

ISACA Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control — elevates the VM program from a scanning function to a formal risk management capability. Vulnerability management practitioners with CRISC can speak directly to board-level risk language — translating CVSS scores into business risk impact. Requires 3 years IT risk management experience for certification.
